chunks.text:
# Manufacturing Business Glossary

This glossary defines the core business concepts and terminology used in the manufacturing supply chain domain. It covers product structures, materials management, production workflows, and quality assurance processes.

## Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex product... [TRUNCATED]

## Warehouse

A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique warehouse identifier
- Warehouse name and location
- Address, city, and ... [TRUNCATED]

## QualityControl

Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods. QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique QC identifier
- Batch reference (pr... [TRUNCATED]

# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

| Column | Da... [TRUNCATED]

## bom

Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| bom_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique BOM identifier |
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) |
| component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → produc... [TRUNCATED]

## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |
| warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |
| address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |
| city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
| state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |
| capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CH... [TRUNCATED]

## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |
| product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |
| parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition... [TRUNCATED]

## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier |
| specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name |
| version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version |
| effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date |
| spec_type | spec_type_t | ... [TRUNCATED]

## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| shipment_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique shipment identifier |
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL |
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference |
| supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | S... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: Ea
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unit of measure representing a single item (each) used in inventory and transactional quantities alongside other units such as KG, M, and L.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) | | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.)

entities_post_resolution.name: Product Produced
entities_post_resolution.definition: Product name is the human-readable label assigned to a product in the product master data, stored as a required text field.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | - A product can contain other products as components via BOM | A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex products can contain simpler products as parts. | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | Products can be active or inactive based on current manufacturing requirements. | A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Products are stored in Inventory records | | product_name | VARCHAR(100)

entities_post_resolution.name: Actual Arrival
entities_post_resolution.definition: A DATE field representing the actual arrival date of a shipment, constrained to be on or after the ship_date.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | actual_arrival | DATE | | Actual arrival date | | - `chk_shipment_dates` — ensures actual_arrival >= ship_date

entities_post_resolution.name: Batch
entities_post_resolution.definition: A production batch (lot) representing a specific run of product units produced together, tracked for traceability and inventory management, recording product, production date, quantity, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | QC status: PENDING, PASSED, FAILED, QUARANTINED | Batches produce a specific Product | Batches are stored in Warehouses | Batches undergo Quality Control inspections | Batches may have expiry dates for perishable goods | Batches enable traceability and recall management | **Relationships:**
- One-to-many with inventory
- One-to-many with shipment
- One-to-many with work_order
- One-to-many with batch | ## batch

Tracks production lots for traceability and inventory management. | | quantity_produced | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK > 0 | Quantity produced |

entities_post_resolution.name: Check >= 0
entities_post_resolution.definition: A SQL CHECK constraint on the Quantity Completed field enforcing the value to be between 0 and the ordered quantity (non-negative and not exceeding the ordered amount).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_completed | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0, <= ordered | Quantity completed | | | quantity_ordered | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK > 0 | Quantity to produce | | | defect_count | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Number of defects found |

entities_post_resolution.name: Component
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Component is an atomic raw material or purchased part used as a building block in manufacturing, procured from external suppliers.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items. | Components are the building blocks used in manufacturing and are procured from external suppliers. | Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) | Technical specification reference | Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many) | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | | component_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Component name | | | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) | | ## component

Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers. | - One-to-many with inventory | - Optional relationship with spec

entities_post_resolution.name: Ship Date, Estimated Arrival
entities_post_resolution.definition: A two-field composite entity representing the shipment date (ship_date) and the estimated arrival date (estimated_arrival) as referenced in the shipments data index.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - `idx_shipment_dates` on (ship_date, estimated_arrival) | | estimated_arrival | DATE | | Estimated arrival date |

entities_post_resolution.name: Finished Good, Assembly, Component
entities_post_resolution.definition: A product type category used in product data governance to classify items as finished goods, assemblies, or components within inventory/ERP systems.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
```sql
product_type_t: ENUM ('FINISHED_GOOD', 'ASSEMBLY', 'COMPONENT')
status_t: ENUM ('PENDING', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED', 'CLOSED')
shipment_type_t: ENUM ('INBOUND', 'OUTBOUND', 'INTERNAL')
shipment_status_t: ENUM ('PENDING', 'SHIPPED', 'DELIVERED', 'CANCELLED')
qc_type_t: ENUM ('INCOMING', 'IN_PROCESS', 'FINAL')
qc_result_t: ENUM ('PASS', 'FAIL', 'CONDITIONAL')
spec_type_t: ENUM ('DIMENSIONAL', 'MATERIAL', 'PERFORMANCE')
qc_status_t: ENUM ('PENDING', 'PASSED', 'FAILED', 'QUARANTINED')
yes_no_t: ENUM ('Y', 'N')
``` | | product_type | product_type_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'FINISHED_GOOD' | Product classification: FINISHED_GOOD, ASSEMBLY, COMPONENT |

entities_post_resolution.name: In Progress Completed Closed
entities_post_resolution.definition: An enumerated lifecycle status set representing the progression of a work item through stages such as pending, in progress, completed, and closed.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | status | status_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'PENDING' | PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CLOSED | | | qc_type | qc_type_t | NOT NULL | INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, FINAL |

entities_post_resolution.name: Inventory
entities_post_resolution.definition: A data entity that records stock levels of components and products at warehouse locations, including quantity_on_hand, quantity_reserved, and reorder_threshold.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- One-to-many with inventory | ## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |
| warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |
| address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |
| city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
| state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |
| capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK > 0 | Storage capacity |
| manager_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Warehouse manager reference |

**Relationships:**
- One-to-many with inventory
- One-to-many with shipment
- One-to-many with work_order
- One-to-many with batch | ## inventory

Tracks stock levels for components and products at specific warehouse locations. | ## inventory

Tracks stock levels for components and products at specific warehouse locations.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|

entities_post_resolution.name: Parent Product Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: A foreign key column that references the parent product's product_id to establish a multi-level product hierarchy and assemblies.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product for hierarchical assemblies | | - `parent_product_id` references `product_id` — enables multi-level product assemblies | - Both `parent_product_id` and `component_product_id` reference the product table | | parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) | | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | ### Product Hierarchy
The `product` table's `parent_product_id` column references `product_id`, enabling multi-level product assemblies: | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture | | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique product identifie

entities_post_resolution.name: Planned Start Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: planned_end_date is the scheduled date by which a task or project is planned to be completed.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | planned_start_date | DATE | | Planned start date | | | planned_end_date | DATE | | Planned end date |

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Shipment is the event representing the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, suppliers, and customers (inbound from suppliers to warehouses and outbound to customers).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL | | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | A Shipment represents the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound). | Key Attributes:
- Unique shipment identifier
- Type: INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL | Shipments connect Warehouses, Suppliers, and Customers | Shipments update Inventory levels | Shipments fulfill Work Order outputs | Warehouses receive and ship Shipments | **Relationships:**
- One-to-many with inventory
- One-to-many with shipment
- One-to-many with work_order
- One-to-many with batch | ## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

entities_post_resolution.name: Technical Specification
entities_post_resolution.definition: A technical specification is a document that defines the technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria governing materials or products.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Technical specification reference | A Specification defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products. | Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure. | Specifications are used in Quality Control inspections | Specifications have version control for engineering changes | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | - Optional relationship with specification | ## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products.

entities_post_resolution.name: Unique Parent Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique constraint on the BOM structure that prevents duplicate entries for a given parent_product_id and component_product_id pair.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Constraints:**
- `uq_bom_structure` UNIQUE (parent_product_id, component_product_id) — prevents duplicate entries | - `uq_component_supplier` UNIQUE (component_id, supplier_id) — prevents duplicate supplier associations

entities_post_resolution.name: Unique Relationship Identifier
entities_post_resolution.definition: An identifier used as the primary key for the component-supplier relationship, uniquely identifying the linkage between a component and its supplier.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | component_supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique relationship identifier | | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique component identifier | | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique product identifier | | | shipment_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique shipment identifier | | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | Key Attributes: - Unique supplier identifier | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique supplier identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Warehouse(Warehouse Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A warehouse is a physical storage facility identified by a unique warehouse_id used to store inventory (materials, components, and finished goods), characterized by its location/address, capacity, and an assigned manager.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
## inventory

Tracks stock levels for components and products at specific warehouse locations.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| inventory_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique inventory identifier |
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse location |
| component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) |
| product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product reference (exclusive with component_id) |
| quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity |
| quantity_reserved | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Reserved quantity |
| reorder_threshold | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00 | Reorder trigger level |
| last_restock_date | DATE | | Last restock timestamp | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse

entities_post_resolution.name: Work Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A work_order is a manufacturing production job used to plan and track production, consuming inventory components and producing products, with support for hierarchical decomposition into sub work orders.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Relationships:**
- One-to-many with inventory
- One-to-many with shipment
- One-to-many with work_order
- One-to-many with batch | ## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support. | | work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier | | **Indexes:**
- `idx_work_order_product` on product_id
- `idx_work_order_parent` on parent_work_order (WHERE NOT NULL)
- `idx_work_order_status` on status
- `idx_work_order_dates` on (planned_start_date, planned_end_date) | **Constraints:**
- `chk_dates_consistent` — ensures end_date >= start_date | - A large production job can be broken into child work orders
- Supports complex production planning and tracking | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Warehouses are linked to Work Orders for production staging | Work Orders produce Products | Work Orders consume Inventory (components) | Work Orders are associated with Warehouses | Work Orders can have hierarchi

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: Product is the business entity representing any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods, sub-assemblies, and components, and may support hierarchical relationships among products.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex products can contain simpler products as parts. | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | Products can be active or inactive based on current manufacturing requirements. | A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Products are stored in Inventory records | ## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

entities_post_resolution.name: Base Manufacturing Cost
entities_post_resolution.definition: The baseline manufacturing cost of a product, used for planning and costing in production and procurement processes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | | base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost |

entities_post_resolution.name: Component Supplier Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: Junction table linking components to their approved suppliers with pricing and lead time information.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| component_supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique relationship identifier | | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique component identifier | | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | | - Both `parent_product_id` and `component_product_id` reference the product table | - Many-to-many with supplier via component_supplier junction table | ## component_supplier
Junction table linking components to their approved suppliers with pricing and lead time information.

entities_post_resolution.name: Product Composition
entities_post_resolution.definition: The product composition defined by the Bill of Materials (BOM), specifying the components and sub-assemblies required to manufacture a parent product and the resulting hierarchical structure.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product. | Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure.

entities_post_resolution.name: Expiry Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The expiry date is the calendar date by which a batch or product should be considered usable or sellable, after which it may be discarded or flagged as expired.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches may have expiry dates for perishable goods

entities_post_resolution.name: External Vendor Information
entities_post_resolution.definition: external vendor information: data records containing details about external vendors that supply materials, components, or finished goods for procurement.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
## supplier
Stores external vendor information for component procurement. | Components are the building blocks used in manufacturing and are procured from external suppliers. | A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization.

entities_post_resolution.name: Idx Shipment Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: An index on the shipment date attributes (ship_date, estimated_arrival) used to accelerate queries filtering or sorting by shipment dates.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - `idx_shipment_status` on status | - `idx_shipment_dates` on (ship_date, estimated_arrival)

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufacturing Workflow
entities_post_resolution.definition: A manufacturing workflow is the defined sequence of manufacturing steps, operations, and routing that governs how a product is produced, used for production scheduling, capacity planning, and cost calculation.
synonyms: manufacturing workflow steps and operations for product production
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Routes collectively define the manufacturing workflow and are used for production scheduling, capacity planning, and cost calculation. | ## route

Defines manufacturing workflow steps and operations for product production.

entities_post_resolution.name: Performance Rating
entities_post_resolution.definition: A numeric performance score on a 1.0 to 5.0 scale used to evaluate and rate supplier performance in procurement operations.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | rating | DECIMAL(3,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 3.00, CHECK 1.0-5.0 | Performance rating | | Suppliers are evaluated based on performance ratings, preferred status for strategic partnerships, and maintain contact information for procurement operations.

entities_post_resolution.name: Physical Storage Location Information
entities_post_resolution.definition: An information artifact that stores details about physical storage locations used to store inventory, supporting inventory management.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management. | A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored.

entities_post_resolution.name: Production Lot
entities_post_resolution.definition: A production lot is a batch representing a specific production run of a product, containing all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle for traceability and inventory management.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | ## batch

Tracks production lots for traceability and inventory management.

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity
entities_post_resolution.definition: A required, positive decimal quantity value stored as DECIMAL(10,3) with a default of 1.000.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity | DECIMAL(10,3) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1.000, CHECK > 0 | Required quantity |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Produced
entities_post_resolution.definition: Quantity produced is the numeric amount of product manufactured in a batch or production run, used in production records to track output.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_produced | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK > 0 | Quantity produced | | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.

entities_post_resolution.name: Standard Cost
entities_post_resolution.definition: Standard_cost is the predefined or baseline procurement cost assigned to a component, used as the target/default cost for budgeting and procurement decisions.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | | Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements.

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier
entities_post_resolution.definition: Supplier is the business entity representing an external vendor that supplies components, identified by a unique supplier_id and described by a supplier_name.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization. | Suppliers are evaluated based on performance ratings, preferred status for strategic partnerships, and maintain contact information for procurement operations. | Suppliers are associated with components they can provide through approved supplier relationships. | Key Attributes: - Unique supplier identifier | Key Attributes: - Supplier name and contact details - Contact email and phone number | Key Attributes: - Performance rating (1.0 to 5.0 scale) - Preferred supplier flag | Suppliers provide multiple Components (many-to-many via component_supplier) | Suppliers are linked to Inbound Shipments | Suppliers have negotiated pricing and lead times per component | - Many-to-many with supplier via component_supplier junction table | ## supplier
Stores external vendor information for component procurement. | | supplier_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Sup

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Of Measure
entities_post_resolution.definition: The unit_of_measure is the standard unit (e.g., EA, KG, M, L) used to quantify items or specifications in the data model.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure | | Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure.

entities_post_resolution.name: Route
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Route represents the defined sequence of manufacturing operations, their associated work centers, and timing parameters (cycle and setup times) required to produce a product.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Route defines the sequence of manufacturing operations or steps required to produce a product. | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number. | Routes collectively define the manufacturing workflow and are used for production scheduling, capacity planning, and cost calculation. | ## route

Defines manufacturing workflow steps and operations for product production. | | sequence_number | INT | NOT NULL, CHECK > 0 | Operation sequence |

entities_post_resolution.name: Acceptable Value Ranges
entities_post_resolution.definition: Acceptance criteria are the specific conditions and pass/fail requirements that determine whether a material or product complies with the defined specification.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure. | A Specification defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products.

entities_post_resolution.name: Atomic Raw Materials
entities_post_resolution.definition: Atomic raw materials and purchased parts are basic, non-decomposable items procured from external suppliers that are stored as components in inventory.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
## component

Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers. | A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items.

entities_post_resolution.name: Procurement Lead Time
entities_post_resolution.definition: Lead time in days for procurement or production, i.e., the duration from initiating a purchase or production to its completion.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0 | Procurement lead time | | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number. | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | Key Attributes: - Performance rating (1.0 to 5.0 scale) - Preferred supplier flag | | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time |

entities_post_resolution.name: Defect Count
entities_post_resolution.definition: An integer field representing the total number of defects detected during a quality-control inspection.

entities_post_resolution.name: Product Type
entities_post_resolution.definition:
```sql
product_type_t: ENUM ('FINISHED_GOOD', 'ASSEMBLY', 'COMPONENT')
status_t: ENUM ('PENDING', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED', 'CLOSED')
shipment_type_t: ENUM ('INBOUND', 'OUTBOUND', 'INTERNAL')
shipment_status_t: ENUM ('PENDING', 'SHIPPED', 'DELIVERED', 'CANCELLED')
qc_type_t: ENUM ('INCOMING', 'IN_PROCESS', 'FINAL')
qc_result_t: ENUM ('PASS', 'FAIL', 'CONDITIONAL')
spec_type_t: ENUM ('DIMENSIONAL', 'MATERIAL', 'PERFORMANCE')
qc_status_t: ENUM ('PENDING', 'PASSED', 'FAILED', 'QUARANTINED')
yes_no_t: ENUM ('Y', 'N')
```

- Child work orders for sub-assemblies or operations
- Hierarchical production planning and tracking

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products including identifiers, names, types, hierarchical relationships between products, base cost, lead time to delivery, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Component
tables_enriched.description: Stores data about individual components, including identifiers, names, categories, measurement units, cost, and associated specification references.

tables_enriched.name: BOM (Bill of Materials)
tables_enriched.description: Stores the relationships between parent products and their component products, including the required quantity and unit of measure for each component within a bill of materials.

tables_enriched.name: Supplier
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about suppliers, including contact details, rating, and whether they are a preferred supplier.

tables_enriched.name: Warehouse
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about each warehouse, including its identifier, name, location (address, city, state), capacity, and the manager responsible for the facility.

tables_enriched.name: Inventory
tables_enriched.description: Stores current inventory levels and restocking indicators for products across warehouses, including on-hand quantities, reserved quantities, and the threshold to trigger reorders.

tables_enriched.name: Work Order
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about work orders including quantities, statuses, priorities, planned dates, and associated product and warehouse references, to manage manufacturing or fulfillment tasks.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments, including identifiers, associated warehouse, supplier, and customer, ship and arrival dates, and current status.

tables_enriched.name: Quality Control
tables_enriched.description: Stores data about quality control checks performed on batches, including the type of check, date, inspector, result, and any defect counts or notes.

tables_enriched.name: Specification
tables_enriched.description: Stores detailed specifications for product or process parameters, including their identifiers, names, versions, effective dates, type, key parameter thresholds, and measurement units.

tables_enriched.name: Batch
tables_enriched.description: Stores batch-level production data, linking each batch to a product and warehouse, including production and expiry dates, quantity produced, and quality control status.

tables_enriched.name: Route
tables_enriched.description: Stores the sequence and timing of operations required to route a product through various work centers, including operation details, center assignment, and the associated cycle and setup times.

tables_enriched.name: Component Supplier
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about supplier components, including preferred status, lead time, and unit price for each component supplied by a supplier.
